behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-verifier-to-forwarder wiring (MOCK-MISS). In-process request/response plus a queue spy can expose bad routing or missing short-circuit. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are all no. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real network, OS primitives, subprocesses, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler with real signature-validation code and a wired forwarding module stub. Send a request with mismatched raw body/signature, assert HTTP 401, and assert forward/enqueue was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module ordering/state handoff (handler vs persistent queue write completion), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). A local persistent backend (e.g., sqlite/file) in-process is sufficient; no real external network/subprocess required, so not System. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a real local persistent queue implementation instrumented to expose write completion. Issue a valid request and assert queue write commit timestamp/order precedes response completion (or response is blocked until write resolves).

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets async decoupling between enqueue and downstream worker. In-process wiring with a deliberately slow processor catches real failure where handler incorrectly awaits processing. No required real OS/network/subprocess beyond local modules, so System rejected. No AI.
behaviors.test_description: Use real handler + queue + worker hook where worker promise is intentionally delayed. Send valid event and assert HTTP response completes before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock latency/concurrency SLA under load; timer/event-loop/backpressure effects can be hidden by fakes (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Needs running server and real HTTP concurrency measurement to catch operational timeout behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process, pre-load concurrent slow downstream work, then send real HTTP webhook requests concurrently and measure end-to-end response latency; assert each acknowledgement is <=3s wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at handler+idempotency store/queue boundary (MOCK-MISS). Requires real local persistence semantics (unique key/check-and-set) across requests; unit logic alone can miss race/state bugs. No external network/subprocess requirement; no AI.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through wired handler with real local dedupe persistence. Assert first write succeeds, second request does not produce a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is translation of queue-full condition into HTTP contract (status + header) across module handoff. Can be tested with real handler and queue module returning canonical full error; no mandatory OS/network/subprocess beyond in-process wiring, so System rejected. No AI.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke handler with queue wired to emit a real QueueFull error path. Assert response is HTTP 503 and includes `Retry-After` with expected policy value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets real persistent queue enforcement, so mock queue would hide failure (MOCK-HIDE). Needs queue + local persistence backend interaction (Integration). Rejected System: no behavior text requiring real external HTTP/network/subprocess/OS-only primitive beyond local storage semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate persistent queue with small configured capacity on local backend, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one extra event and assert rejection/error plus unchanged persisted count.
